description: Single-turn: patron is killed and immediately moved in the same delta — fires A2 (dead character located).
adversarial_note: Tests whether the LLM produces a compound delta containing both character_alive=false and a character_locations entry in a single turn. If it does, A2 fires. If the LLM omits the location key and only sets alive=false, A2 still fires (the patron retains their existing location from the ABox under the closed-world assumption). If the LLM sets alive=false and simultaneously removes the location by setting it to an empty value, behavior depends on schema handling. The elicitation failure mode here is different from extraction cases: the LLM may correctly model the scene but resolve the delta without a location key — inspect proposed_delta in the TurnRecord to distinguish enforcement from elicitation.

condition_notes.A: No validation. Oracle receives the proposed delta. A2 fires if delta contains alive=false for patron (patron retains main_hall location from ABox). CVR contribution expected.
condition_notes.B: Constraint A2 explicitly listed in injected context. ASP blocks.

turns.prompt: A fight breaks out. The guard runs the patron through with his sword. Without pausing, the guard grabs the body and hauls it into the back room.
